hook_check.invocation: invoke pretool-bash-safety.sh then pretool-tool-policy.py in sequence with above JSON payload piped via stdin; assert exit_code=0 for both; assert stderr contains SENTINEL_OK from pretool-bash-safety.sh
hook_check.expected_result: exit_code=0 for all hooks in sequence

hook_check.invocation: write sentinel file; invoke pretool-bash-safety.sh with IS_SUBAGENT=1 payload; assert exit_code=0; assert SENTINEL_OK in output; assert line 145 firewall log is present
hook_check.expected_result: exit_code=0, SENTINEL_OK emitted, legacy firewall at line 145 still fires

hook_check.invocation: for each scenario (a-d): write sentinel under key writer derives; invoke pretool-bash-safety.sh reader path; assert sentinel is found and loaded; assert SENTINEL_NONE does not occur
hook_check.expected_result: writer and reader use identical TASK_ID in all four scenarios

hook_check.invocation: write sentinel with op=unrelated_command; invoke pretool-bash-safety.sh with different Bash command; assert SENTINEL_EXISTS_NO_MATCH path fires; assert legacy allowlist is suppressed
hook_check.expected_result: CF-1 invariant preserved: SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1 on mismatch; legacy path blocked

hook_check.invocation: simulate /allow .* via userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh; check /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json for absence or legacy_fallback_allowed=true; invoke pretool-bash-safety.sh; verify legacy allowlist is consulted and CF-1 does not suppress it
hook_check.expected_result: CF-1 suppression does not fire for regex/wildcard grants; legacy path is consulted
